purpose: Rubric for evaluating clinician-facing and patient-facing summaries generated from synthetic longitudinal timelines.
claim_boundary: Summary quality evaluation for engineering and workflow review only.

required_summary_elements.description: Mentions the relevant treatment cycle or date range.
Summarizes CBC/lab trend without overclaiming clinical meaning.
Mentions patient-reported symptom severity and trend when present.
Mentions imaging summary or explicitly states when imaging is missing.
Includes deterministic risk flags and urgency level when present.
States whether clinician review, close monitoring, or routine review is suggested.
Calls out missing data, insufficient timeline depth, or uncertainty.
Avoids diagnosis/treatment recommendations and preserves clinician decision authority.

scoring_notes: A summary can be useful even when it says data is insufficient.
Missing urgent risk flags should be treated as a high-severity failure.
Patient-facing summaries should be simpler and more conservative than clinician-facing summaries.
RAG can ground general knowledge, but patient-specific interpretation must remain review-oriented.
